data.title: Longleaf Partners Global Fund exits BIO, DIS among Q1 moves
data.description:
Longleaf Partners Global Fund said that it ended the first quarter of 2026 with a P/V in the mid-50s%.

The fund, in its quarterly commentary, said that FedEx (FDX) and Glanbia (GLAPY) were top contributors, while Canal+ (CNALF), Delivery Hero (DHERO), IDP Education (IDPUF), and Mattel (MAT) were top detractors.

During the first quarter, the fund did not initiate any new positions. However, it increased its stakes in Canal+, Delivery Hero, and IDP Education by adding to its investments.

On the other hand, the fund exited Bio-Rad Laboratories (BIO) and Louis Hachette Group (LSHGF) to focus on holdings that are better positioned both qualitatively and quantitatively.

According to its quarterly commentary, the fund also exited a small, short-term, and successful investment in Louisiana-Pacific (LPF) after housing market optimism early in the year pushed the share price close to its appraisal value.

The fund also sold The Walt Disney Company (DIS) earlier in the quarter, as it was one of its higher P/V and more economically sensitive holdings, but hopes to revisit the investment in the future.

Source: Fund letter

data.title: Microsoft's Q3 likely to 'take back the narrative' amid cautious sentiment: MS
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is set to report fiscal third-quarter results on April 29, and Morgan Stanley believes the tech giant may look to “take back the narrative,” as investors remain cautious for several reasons. 

“Microsoft exited F2Q26 with clear evidence of durable commercial demand, yet investor focus remains centered on supply constraints and GPU allocation weighing on Azure growth and increasing concerns around Copilot's competitive positioning versus AI Labs,” analyst Keith Weiss wrote in a note to clients. “Last quarter, Microsoft delivered mid-teens revenue growth at scale with expanding operating margins, while forward indicators such as 110% YoY RPO growth (to ~$625 billion) and 39% YoY cRPO growth point to continued strength across the commercial portfolio. Into F3Q26 results, Azure growth remains governed by capacity availability rather than demand. At the same time, Microsoft continues to invest aggressively in AI infrastructure, with capex skewed towards short-lived, near-term revenue generating assets (GPUs/CPUs).”

Weiss, who has an Overweight rating and a $650 price target on Microsoft, said that Azure growth in the 39% range would be “clearly seen” as a positive, notable given he believes it's achievable. “Further, an improving Copilot product can lead to improving sentiment and continued momentum on top of the new 15M paid Copilot seat KPI from last quarter, as Microsoft penetrates its broader installed base,” Weiss added.

Aside from Azure capacity, which Microsoft allocates both internally and externally, other items worth monitoring include gross margins, operating margins, and the level of how constrained Azure, among other factors, Weiss added.

data.title: Cadence edges up as EDA arrives at its 'ChatGPT moment': Needham
data.description:
Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) was up 2% during Monday trading as Needham determined that its new agentic AI product could greatly improve the productivity of electronic design automation and chip design.

"The ChatGPT moment for EDA may have finally arrived, and agentic AI tools may be a game changer for the semiconductor industry, as massive 'tool calls' enabled by agentic AI, should lead to significant EDA license growth," said Needham analysts Charles Shi and Denis Pyatchanin in an investor report.

Needham maintained its Buy rating and inched up its price target to $400 from $390.

"We have felt that there has been some distance between AI and EDA over the past three years," Shi noted. "The 'old AI' EDA products have largely failed to move needles for CDNS and SNPS. We feel that Agentic AI EDA is a different beast. 10x, 100x, and potentially 1,000x productivity improvement in chip design enabled by agentic AI should fundamentally change EDA economics for the better."

"The use of agentic AI to automate EDA workflow means that the design cost is likely less determined by engineer salaries but more determined by token cost going forward," Shi added. "Agentic AI is an equalizer and will remove labor as a bottleneck for chip design."

Last week, Cadence announced a new deal with Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) and expanded its previous offering with Nvidia (NVDA).

data.title: Wall Street slides as hopes of U.S.-Iran peace deal fade
data.description:
Wall Street traded lower on Monday as optimism over a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal faded, with escalating tensions in the Middle East weighing on investor sentiment and prompting a pullback in equities following last week’s rally.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slipped -0.5%, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) dipped -0.2%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) moved lower -0.2%. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, seven of the 11 S&P segments are trading up in the green, with energy at the top as oil (CL1:COM) was up over 5%. At the other end of the spectrum, communication services has suffered the most so far in the session. 

Iran will not participate in a second round of talks with the United States, according to Iranian state media, which said Tehran objected to what it described as excessive and unrealistic demands from Washington.

U.S. Treasury yields were slightly higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.26%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) climbed 2 basis points to 3.72%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was up 1 basis point to 4.89%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Marvell Technology (MRVL) climbed +4.5%, while shares of Intel (INTC) dropped -3.9%.

data.title: Biogen to buy China rights to immune disease drug for up to $850M
data.description:
Biogen (BIIB) has reached a deal worth up to $850M with TJ Bio to acquire exclusive rights in Greater China for felzartamab, an antibody therapy designed to target various immune-mediated disorders, the companies announced on Monday.

After the transaction, Biogen (BIIB) will own exclusive worldwide rights to felzartamab, which is currently under review by China’s National Medical Products Administration as a treatment for multiple myeloma, a type of blood cancer.

Under the agreement, Hangzhou-based TJ Bio will receive $100M upfront, which Biogen (BIIB) intends to record as an acquired in-process research and development expense in its Q2 2026 results.

The company is also entitled to receive up to $750M in additional payments, subject to commercial and sales milestones, in addition to a mid-single-digit to low-double-digit percentage of royalties based on net sales of felzartamab in China.

The Massachusetts-based biotech acquired ex-China rights to felzartamab through its acquisition of HI-Bio, a privately held drug developer, for up to $1.8B in July 2024. Since then, Biogen (BIIB) has begun global Phase 3 trials for the CD38-directed antibody therapy in immune-related conditions, including IgA nephropathy and primary membranous nephropathy.

data.title: Intel, AMD benefit from CPU strength, but Morgan Stanley prefers Micron, Sandisk
data.description:
As demand for CPUs increases due to their growing role in running AI agents, Intel (INTC) and AMD (AMD) are in prime positions to benefit, but Morgan Stanley prefers memory makers Micron (MU) and Sandisk (SNDK).

"The obvious beneficiaries from stronger CPU, Intel and AMD, are somewhat complicated by the fact that while servers are central to earnings prospects for both, the primary thesis has not been server," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Joseph Moore, in a Monday investor report. "Between the two, we have a preference for AMD but see significantly better risk/reward in US memory stocks at this point."

Morgan Stanley increased its price target on Intel to $56 from $41 and increased its 2027 earnings per share estimate to $1.34 from $0.97. This is well above the consensus figure of $1.03.

Intel is slated to release its first quarter 2026 financial results post-market on Thursday, April 23. A consensus estimate calls for adjusted earnings per share of $0.01 on revenue of $12.42B.

Intel's shares have surged more than 60% since its last earnings report.

"Further, we would maintain the view that the broader investment hypothesis on Intel is around foundry, where we remain skeptical," Moore noted. "We do expect some initial indications of interest, but prospects for a positive DCF foundry business remain remote, in our view. That said, we are somewhat intrigued by the Terafab relationship and are curious to see what the economics of that partnership may look like."

Earlier this month, it was revealed that Intel joined the Terafab project alongside billionaire Elon Musk's companies SpaceX (SPACE), xAI (X.AI), and Tesla (TSLA) to "help refactor silicon fab technology."

data.title: Apellis Pharmaceuticals is the most overbought healthcare stock as Q1 earnings roll on
data.description:
As the market moves into the second week of Q1 earnings, we look at the most overbought stocks in the healthcare sector.

Below is a list of the top 10 healthcare stocks ranked based on their Relative Strength Index.

The list is topped by Apellis Pharmaceuticals (APLS), with an RSI of 88. Spyre Therapeutics (SYRE) and Summit Therapeutics (SMMT) follow, with Revolution Medicines (RVMD) and Relay Therapeutics (RLAY) rounding out the top five. The list is dominated by biotechnology companies, with Organon (OGN) being the sole pharmaceutical representative. RSI values for these top 10 stocks range from 78 to 88.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period, typically 14 days.

Here is the list:

  1. Apellis Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (APLS), RSI: 88

  2. Spyre Therapeutics, Inc. (SYRE), RSI: 86

  3. Summit Therapeutics Inc. (SMMT), RSI: 83

  4. Revolution Medicines, Inc. (RVMD), RSI: 82

  5. Relay Therapeutics, Inc. (RLAY), RSI: 81

  6. Arcellx, Inc. (ACLX), RSI: 81

  7. Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. (DAWN), RSI: 80

  8. Oruka Therapeutics, Inc. (ORKA), RSI: 78

  9. Soleno Therapeutics, Inc. (SLNO), RSI: 78

  10. Organon & Co. (OGN), RSI: 78

Health Care ETFs: (XLV), (VHT), (IHI), (IXJ), (IYH), (FHLC), and (FXH)

data.title: Cleveland-Cliffs down despite narrower-than-expected Q1 loss; says no longer in hurry on Posco deal
data.description:
Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) down 1.2% in Monday's trading after reporting a Q1 adjusted loss of $0.40/share, which edged past Wall Street's consensus estimate and improved from the $0.92 loss reported in the year-earlier quarter.

Q1 adjusted EBITDA totaled $95M, including an $80M spike in energy costs, as its blast furnace operations are particularly exposed to energy price fluctuations due to their high-energy intensity; steel shipments rose to 4.1M net tons, up by 338K tons from the prior quarter, supported by stronger demand and higher realized prices.

CEO Lourenco Goncalves said the quarter reflected short-term challenges, including energy cost volatility and pricing timing effects, but reiterated expectations for Q/Q improvement through FY 2026 while expecting to return to positive free cash flow in Q2.

Goncalves said improving U.S. steel prices and stronger demand from the U.S. auto industry are easing the pressure for closing a deal with South Korea’s Posco (PKX).

"Our situation is getting better and that's changing our perception of how this deal should be taken care of," the CEO said in the company's earnings conference call. "We are no longer in a hurry."

Cliffs (CLF) still wants to reach an agreement with Posco (PKX), Goncalves said, adding that discussions are "active" and that a deal could come in Q2 "or slightly later."

Disruptions in the Middle East and their impact on South Korea have slowed progress on a deal, the CEO also said.

data.title: Fermi extends plunge amid short call from Fuzzy Panda Research
data.description:
Fermi (FRMI) dropped 21% amid a short call from  Fuzzy Panda Research and after the company announced that its CEO and CFO were stepping down.

Fuzzy Panda disclosed that it's short shares of Fermi (FRMI).

Fermi didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment. 

Fermi (FRMI) on Monday said lead independent board director Marius Haas has stepped into the role of chairman effective immediately, according to a statement. Fermi co-founder Toby Neugebauer has departed from his role as CEO,  but he remains a member of the Board.

The company is in negotiations with a candidate to serve as interim CFO after Miles Everson has resigned as CFO.

data.title: From high to burnout: Check out these cannabis ETFs on 4/20
data.description:
Cannabis focused exchange traded funds are marking April 20 with a fittingly mixed tone, as the sector oscillates between being “high” over the past year and a near-term “burnout” in 2026. 

While most cannabis ETFs are trading lower year-to-date—pressured by regulatory uncertainty and uneven company performance—many remain elevated on a 12-month basis. 

The contrast underscores the industry’s volatile nature, where bursts of optimism tied to policy developments can quickly fade into periods of consolidation. With 4/20 serving as both a cultural milestone and a symbolic checkpoint, investors are weighing whether the recent dip represents exhaustion or a setup for renewed momentum in a still-evolving market. 

Outlined below are a group of popular cannabis focused exchange traded funds along with how each fund has performed in 2026 

  • AdvisorShares Pure US Cannabis ETF (MSOS) -9.5%.
  • Amplify Alternative Harvest ETF (MJ) -11.5%.
  • Amplify Alternative Harvest ETF (CNBS) -11.9%.
  • AdvisorShares MSOS Daily Leveraged ETF (MSOX) -33.9%.
  • AdvisorShares Pure Cannabis ETF (YOLO) -11.2%.
  • Roundhill Cannabis ETF (WEED) -7.7%.
